reasoning: As of May 21, 2025, Climate.gov is still up and reachable, having survived the initial and most aggressive wave of data and website removals directed by the Trump administration and DOGE over the last four months. The addition of a 'Statutory Authority' section citing the National Climate Program Act of 1978 provides strong legal cover against a unilateral takedown, and green groups are already actively suing to reinstate other removed environmental sites, making the administration warier of blatant, legally dubious deletions. Furthermore, major structural dismantling (like eliminating NOAA’s Office of Oceanic and Atmospheric Research) relies on the FY2026 budget process, which takes effect primarily in October 2025. Given the short horizon (~40 days until the July 1, 2025 resolution date) and the legal, statutory, and logistical hurdles to completely killing the domain, I estimate an 89% chance that Climate.gov will still resolve as UP on IsItDownRightNow.
